prevalence.source: ICMR and WHO diarrheal disease context
prevalence.trend: decreasing in children with vaccination and sanitation, remains high in vulnerable areas

burden.dalyPerCapita: high in children due to dehydration risk
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate in vulnerable populations

vulnerablePopulations: low-income rural and urban slum populations
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; stool testing for severe, persistent, or outbreak cases

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions of episodes annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on early ORS and care access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; lab testing mainly in outbreaks or severe cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ORS, zinc in children, IV fluids for severe dehydration; antibiotics only when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Sanitation gaps and delayed care in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide ORS availability and strong child health programs
